---
title: "Yannick Loth"
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: github
      href: https://github.com/YannickLoth
    - text: DOI
      href: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19627450
    - icon: rss
      href: blog/index.xml
---

## About

Ing. Yannick Loth, M.Sc. (Management)

This work synthesises the scientific literature on Myalgic Encephalomyelitis / Chronic Fatigue Syndrome with structured epistemic rigour — separating what is established from what is hypothesised, speculated, or unknown. Every claim is classified as an achievement, hypothesis, speculation, prediction, warning, or recommendation, with an explicit certainty rating.

## Motivation

The medical literature on ME/CFS is scattered across thousands of papers, often contradictory, and rarely synthesised in a way that serves both clinicians and patients. This documentation exists to bridge that gap — to make the evidence accessible, structured, and honest about its limits.

## Methodology

The paper uses a multi-layer approach:

- **Structured epistemic classification** — every statement tagged by evidence level
- **AI-assisted literature synthesis** — with mandatory human critical oversight
- **Adversarial review** — multiple AI personas attack the content from hostile angles
- **Formal modeling** — ordinary differential equations for disease dynamics
- **Open source** — all content, code, and build tooling publicly available

## License

All content is released under [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).
